name: CHERI-Lite — Lower-Cost Capability-Style Mitigation
summary: Microsoft Research 2025 proposal exploring a middle ground between full CHERI capabilities and lighter-weight pointer-tagging designs such as MTE. Retains tagged, non-forgeable pointers while reducing the hardware and software adoption burden of full architectural capabilities. Research-stage proposal as of 2026-04.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**CHERI-Lite** (Microsoft Research, 2025) explores a middle ground between full CHERI capabilities and lighter-weight pointer-tagging designs such as MTE. The core idea is to retain the security value of *tagged, non-forgeable pointers* while reducing the hardware and software adoption burden of full architectural capabilities.

Instead of carrying the full CHERI semantic model — bounds, permissions, sealed object capabilities, and a pure-capability ABI — CHERI-Lite focuses on preventing attackers from freely fabricating pointers and reusing stale ones in the most common exploit patterns.

sections.title: Design point — transition strategy
Status
sections.content: Status (as of 2026-04): research-stage proposal, not a mainstream deployed hardware target.

sources.label: CHERI-Lite for Memory Safety Exploit Mitigation
